summary: Employee One (1234) locked out of 2FA despite no 2FA configured — likely SEC-001 sibling bug
detail: Employee One (PIN 1234) attempted PIN login at 07:32:24. System responded with 2fa_required even though users.json shows totp_enabled=false, totp_secret=null. User submitted 5 rapid 2FA codes (07:32:30-07:32:43) all failing 'invalid_code', leading to 15min account lockout. The '2fa_login_success' event at 07:32:30 may indicate the PIN auth passed but was mis-logged. This is the same 2FA state persistence bug pattern as SEC-001 — system remembers 2FA is needed but the secret/key state was lost. Rate limiting worked correctly (locked at 5 attempts).

summary: 2FA state not persisted in users.json despite successful verification
detail: Owner (PIN 1111) successfully verified 2FA TOTP code at 07:13:35 UTC (backup_codes_count: 8). Activity log confirms '2fa_verify_success' event. However, users.json still shows totp_enabled=False, totp_secret=null. The 2FA setup appears to save correctly on initiation (totp_secret written), but the final verification save (setting totp_enabled=True) is lost or overwritten. Possible race condition or another process overwriting users.json. Owner may believe 2FA is active when it is not.
